Feature Name: Sponsor Licence Task Chain Seeding (Fresh App)
Description: Seeding engine expected 6 tasks but got 2 on a fresh app. The roleAutoCompletes logic enforces that caseworker/admin review tasks must be manually completed by a human, whereas the test suite assumes all roles auto-complete when the stage data is complete.

The ensureStageTasks idempotency test throws TypeError: tenantDb.LicenceStageTask.update is not a function. This is caused by the test database mock (makeChainDb) failing to define the update method on the mocked LicenceStageTask model.

Feature Name: Sponsor Licence Task Chain Caseworker Seeding
Description: Fails with AssertionError: Expected stage 2 caseworker task to be seeded. The error is due to the test database mock throwing an error when ensureStageTasks invokes the update method on LicenceStageTask, crashing the seeding execution flow.

toPublicAssetUrl test fails because it expects an absolute URL with 'http://localhost:5000/api/public/images/uuid_123.png', but the service was deprecated/refactored to return a relative URL ('api/public/images/uuid_123.png') to let the frontend resolve the asset URL dynamically.

softDeleteWorker: worker is excluded from standard queries after deletion fails with TypeError: fn is not a function. This happens because the softDeleteWorker service uses Sequelize unmanaged transactions, but the test mock database only supports callback-style managed transactions.

restoreWorker: worker reappears in standard queries after restore fails with TypeError: fn is not a function. This is caused by the restoreWorker service invoking the mock transaction method without a callback parameter.

softDeleteWorker and restoreWorker each write an audit row fails with TypeError: fn is not a function. The error originates from the mock transaction handler which does not support parameterless invocations.

The SMTP settings tab lacks a field for SMTP Sender Email (From address). The backend falls back to using the username/user email as the sender address, which can lead to mail delivery failures if the SMTP provider requires a specific authorized sender address.

The show/hide password toggle (eye icon) is reported as non-functional. The Input component implements lucide-react icons (Eye, EyeOff) which might conflict with react-icons/ri icons used elsewhere, or suffer from z-index/click capture issues.
New badge indicators are displayed properly for new enquiries. Once approved and assigned, status badges update cleanly to 'Under Review' or 'Approved' without visual regression.
Fixed caseworkers filter and action trigger. Correctly lists caseworkers and updates their status from 'Assign' to the name of the assigned caseworker.
Backend search queries and pagination controls are working correctly across lists, solving previous performance bottlenecks.
File upload boundaries and size limits are verified. Verified documents render a green success checkmark in the timeline tracker.
Multi-step licence application wizard saves progress, validates companies house registration, and advances workflow correctly.
Idempotent event processing successfully filters out duplicate webhook signals, preventing double-billing on candidate account activations.
